Executive Summary

  • Engine Stability Crisis: V1 engine crashes, deadlocks, and EngineDeadError failures are blocking the Model Runner V2 default rollout milestone and threatening production reliability.
  • Frontier Model Breakage: Critical day-0 failures across Gemma 4, DeepSeek V3.2, and Qwen3.5 violate the roadmap's commitment to frontier model support and damage enterprise confidence.
  • Blackwell Compatibility Gap: Missing RTX 50-series support spans installation, large-scale serving, and quantization SIGs, blocking adoption of NVIDIA's latest consumer and enterprise hardware.
  • Tool Calling Regression: Gemma 4 parser corruption and widespread structured output failures create critical user-facing regressions that compromise production AI application reliability.
  • Performance Degradation: Significant throughput regressions on flagship models like Qwen3.5-397B (-19%) and Gemma 4 (~90% slowdown) undermine vLLM's performance leadership positioning.
  • Quantization Instability: FP8/NVFP4 quantization producing garbage output across MoE architectures threatens cost-efficiency gains and enterprise deployment viability.
▾

#1 Core Engine

75 issues 18 clusters

System crashes, deadlocks, and EngineDeadError issues directly block the critical Model Runner V2 default rollout milestone and affect fundamental engine stability.

# Problem Related Issues Tags
1 V1 engine EngineDeadError/system crashes with multiprocessing under load — blocks model runner V2 default rollout #37525 Qwen3.5-35B kills workers repeatedly with EngineDeadError on 4xA800 80GB
[Bug]: 启动qwen3.5-35B后反复kill进程：vllm.v1.engine exceptions enginedeaderror enginecore encountered an issue
Problem: Qwen3.5-35B model on vLLM 0.17.1 with 4×80G A800 GPUs repeatedly crashes with 'vllm.v1.engine exceptions enginedeaderror enginecore encountered an issue' and kills processes.
Suggested Fix: Check EngineCore worker stability and memory allocation for multi-GPU Qwen3.5-35B setup. Investigate GPU memory fragmentation, NCCL communication errors, or tensor parallel weight distribution issues. Review logs for OOM conditions or CUDA context corruption that could cause worker process crashes.
Created: 2026-03-19 · View on GitHub →
#37900 Engine V1 crash with WorkerProc leaked shared_memory on multi-GPU setup
[Bug]: Engine V1 crash with WorkerProc leaked shared_memory on multi-GPU setup
Problem: Engine V1 crashes on multi-GPU setup with WorkerProc leaked shared_memory error. The crash occurs during engine initialization or operation in distributed environments.
Suggested Fix: Investigate shared memory cleanup in multi-GPU worker processes. Check for proper cleanup of shared memory segments when workers are terminated or restarted, and ensure proper synchronization between worker processes and shared memory lifecycle management.
Created: 2026-03-23 · View on GitHub →
#35104 V1 engine workers die after idle period (SystemError: PyCFunction / EngineDeadError)
[Bug]: V1 engine workers die after idle period (SystemError: PyCFunction / EngineDeadError) — TP=2, multiprocessing
Problem: V1 engine workers spontaneously die after idle periods with SystemError: PyCFunction / EngineDeadError using GLM-4.7-Flash-AWQ with TP=2 multiprocessing on Docker/Windows.
Suggested Fix: Investigate process cleanup and resource management during idle periods. Check for memory leaks, improper cleanup of multiprocessing resources, or timeout-related issues in the V1 engine worker lifecycle.
Created: 2026-02-23 · View on GitHub →
#35193 Deadlock in async_scheduling with TP=1 (UniProcExecutor) when DP rank raises exception
[Bug]: Deadlock in async_scheduling with TP=1 (UniProcExecutor) when a DP rank raises exception inside `execute_model`
Problem: Deadlock occurs in async_scheduling with TP=1 (UniProcExecutor) when a DP rank raises an exception inside execute_model, because exceptions are wrapped in Future objects but not checked in the same scheduling step.
Suggested Fix: Add exception checking in the EngineCore scheduling loop to detect and handle wrapped exceptions from UniProcExecutor workers. Implement proper exception propagation to prevent indefinite hanging when workers fail.
Created: 2026-02-24 · View on GitHub →
#38428 V1 Engine EngineDeadError (AssertionError) on max_model_len overflow during realtime audio streaming
[Bug]: V1 Engine: EngineDeadError (AssertionError) on max_model_len overflow during realtime audio streaming
Problem: V1 engine crashes with EngineDeadError (AssertionError) when max_model_len is exceeded during realtime audio streaming with mistralai/Voxtral-Mini-4B-Realtime-2602, instead of gracefully terminating with finish_reason='length'.
Suggested Fix: Modify the V1 scheduler to handle max_model_len overflow gracefully in realtime scenarios. Replace the hard assertion with proper error handling that sets finish_reason='length' and terminates the specific request without crashing the entire engine.
Created: 2026-03-28 · View on GitHub →
#33319 Requests stuck in waiting queue with zero running
[Bug]: Requests Stuck in Waiting Queue with Zero Running
Problem: Requests get stuck in waiting queue with zero running requests on vLLM, causing a deadlock condition on Ubuntu 22.04.5 with PyTorch 2.8.0+cu128.
Suggested Fix: Investigate the scheduler state management and request lifecycle. Check for race conditions in request state transitions or deadlocks in the async scheduling logic.

2 CUDA illegal memory access crashes in GDN attention backend with Qwen3.5 MTP — regression from v0.17.0 #34948 Qwen3.5 CUDA Illegal Memory Access in GDN Kernel on H200

Suggested Fix: Investigate memory boundaries and alignment in the GDN kernel implementation. Check if tensor sharding for TP=8 causes out-of-bounds access in the linear attention computation and verify that memory allocation sizes match the expected tensor dimensions for distributed execution.

Problem: GDN attention backend crashes with AssertionError during mixed decode/speculative decode batches when serving Qwen3.5 models with MTP speculative decoding. The assertion failure at line 310 in gdn_attn.py kills the engine and fails all in-flight requests.
Suggested Fix: Fix the batch composition logic in GDN attention backend to properly handle mixed decode and speculative decode requests. The assertion at line 310 likely checks for incompatible batch states that need to be resolved before calling the attention kernel.

#38196 GDN attention backend crashes with ngram speculative decoding on mixed decode batches
GDN attention backend crashes with ngram speculative decoding on mixed decode batches
Problem: GDN (Gated Delta Network) attention backend crashes with AssertionError when ngram speculative decoding produces batches containing both regular decode tokens and speculative decode tokens on Qwen3.5 models.
Suggested Fix: The GDN attention backend doesn't support mixed decode/speculative decode batches. Modify the assertion in gdn_attn.py line 310 to handle mixed batches, or update the speculative decoding scheduler to separate regular and speculative tokens into different batches when using GDN backend.

#35465 No available shared memory broadcast block found in 60 seconds during compilation/weight loading
[Bug]: No available shared memory broadcast block found in 60 seconds. This typically happens when some processes are hanging or doing some time-consuming work (e.g. compilation, weight/kv cache quantization)
Problem: vLLM fails with 'No available shared memory broadcast block found in 60 seconds' timeout error. This typically indicates hanging processes during compilation, weight loading, or KV cache quantization operations.
Suggested Fix: Increase shared memory timeout for large models, investigate which process is hanging (check logs for compilation/loading progress), and ensure all worker processes are responding. Consider reducing parallelism or enabling verbose logging to identify the bottleneck.
Created: 2026-02-27 · View on GitHub →
#36755 Preemption + async scheduling race can corrupt prompt-token accounting and crash Prometheus counters
[Bug] Preemption + async scheduling race can corrupt prompt-token accounting and crash Prometheus counters
Problem: vLLM V1 engine with async scheduling and preemption crashes with 'Counters can only be incremented by non-negative amounts' in Prometheus metrics. The crash occurs when preemption incompletely resets request state, leaving stale num_cached_tokens while resetting num_computed_tokens to 0.
Suggested Fix: Fix _preempt_request() to reset both request.num_computed_tokens and request.num_cached_tokens to 0. Add validation to ensure all token counting fields are properly reset during preemption and investigate race conditions in async scheduling that could corrupt prompt token accounting.

8 KV cache memory overestimation and OOM failures for hybrid Mamba/attention models #37121 KV cache ~7x memory overestimation for hybrid Mamba/attention models (Qwen3.5)
[Performance]: KV cache ~7x memory overestimation for hybrid Mamba/attention models (Qwen3.5)
Problem: Qwen3.5 hybrid Mamba/attention models suffer ~7x KV cache memory overestimation because vLLM treats all layers uniformly. Mamba layers use O(1) state but are allocated the same memory as attention's O(n) KV cache.
Suggested Fix: Modify kv_cache_utils.py to handle per-group memory requirements. Implement separate allocation logic for Mamba (constant state) vs attention (scaling KV) layers in get_max_concurrency_for_kv_cache_config and unify_kv_cache_spec_page_size.

#33704 LoRA dtype mismatch in XPU and CPU punica wrappers
[Bug]: LoRA dtype mismatch in XPU and CPU punica wrappers
Problem: LoRA implementations in XPU and CPU punica wrappers hardcode torch.float32 for buffers regardless of input tensor dtype, causing mismatches with bfloat16 models.
Suggested Fix: Update punica_gpu.py and punica_xpu.py to use x.dtype instead of torch.float32 for buffer allocation. The original Triton constraint requiring float32 has been resolved in recent versions.

18 Advanced KV cache compression and management features #39076 Entropy-Gated Online KV Block Expiration During Active Decode
[RFC]: Entropy-Gated Online KV Block Expiration During Active Decode
Problem: This is an RFC proposing entropy-gated online KV block expiration during active decode, allowing runtime eviction of low-attention KV cache blocks instead of whole-request preemption under memory pressure.
Suggested Fix: Design the proposed entropy-based KV cache eviction system by implementing attention weight monitoring, entropy calculation during decode, and runtime block eviction mechanisms. This requires extending BlockSpaceManager with attention feedback channels and provably bounded error guarantees.

#38694 O(1) KV Cache for vLLM: 4.8x Speedup & 22x More Accurate than TurboQuant on Qwen2.5-7B
[RFC]: O(1) KV Cache for vLLM: 4.8x Speedup & 22x More Accurate than TurboQuant on Qwen2.5-7B
Problem: RFC proposes Nuclear ZFC (NZFC) architecture claiming O(1) KV cache complexity with 4.8x speedup and 22x better accuracy than TurboQuant. The proposal suggests using trace-class admissibility and bounded prototype memory instead of full KV cache materialization.

Feature Request
▾

#2 Model Support

58 issues 16 clusters

Multiple frontier model failures (Gemma 4, DeepSeek V3.2, Qwen3.5) violate the day-0 accuracy validation commitment and directly impact enterprise adoption.

# Problem Related Issues Tags
1 Gemma 4 MoE model family fails at startup with AssertionError: top_k is None in MoEMixin.recursive_replace — v0.19.0 regression #39066 Gemma 4 MoE (26B-A4B-it) crashes at startup — AssertionError: top_k is None in MoEMixin.recursive_replace
[Bug]: Gemma 4 MoE (26B-A4B-it) crashes at startup — AssertionError: top_k is None in MoEMixin.recursive_replace
Problem: Gemma 4 MoE (26B-A4B-it) crashes at startup with AssertionError: top_k is None in MoEMixin.recursive_replace. Gemma4TextConfig uses 'top_k_experts' attribute but vLLM looks for 'num_experts_per_tok' or 'top_k'.
Suggested Fix: Add 'top_k_experts' to the getattr_iter search list in MoEMixin.recursive_replace. Update the attribute lookup to include Gemma4's naming convention: ['num_experts_per_tok', 'top_k', 'top_k_experts'].

Problem: GLM-5 (GlmMoeDsaForCausalLM) with MXFP4 quantization crashes during decode phase on ROCm MI355x with ZeroDivisionError or Memory access fault. MLA decode kernel requires num_heads >= 16 but receives 8 heads at TP=8, and FP8 paged MQA logits causes division by zero when heads < 64.

16 Configuration parameter support missing for various models including Phi qk_layernorm, GPT scale_attn_weights, and OLMoE clip_qkv #37852 Phi qk_layernorm appears to be unsupported in vLLM
[Bug]: Phi qk_layernorm appears to be unsupported in vLLM
Problem: Phi models with config.qk_layernorm=True are unsupported in vLLM. The Transformers implementation applies q_layernorm and k_layernorm per-head before rotary embedding, but vLLM's Phi implementation skips this step entirely.
Suggested Fix: Add support for qk_layernorm in vllm/model_executor/models/phi.py. Check config.qk_layernorm during initialization, create q_layernorm/k_layernorm modules if enabled, and apply them to query_states/key_states before the rotary_emb call.

Problem: Disaggregated prefill script fails due to inconsistent request IDs between prefill and decode nodes. The request ID mismatch prevents proper communication between P and D nodes in disaggregated serving setup.
Suggested Fix: Ensure request ID synchronization between prefill and decode nodes. Check the request ID generation and transfer logic in the disaggregated serving protocol, and verify that both nodes use consistent ID mapping for request tracking.
Created: 2026-04-02 · View on GitHub →
#37745 Mooncake Connector: Decode nodes stuck in WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS after Prefill node restart
[Bug]: Mooncake Connector: Decode nodes stuck in WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS after Prefill node restart
Problem: Mooncake Connector decode nodes get stuck in WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS state after a prefill node restarts, creating a distributed system deadlock where decode nodes wait indefinitely for remote key-value cache data.
Suggested Fix: Implement proper state recovery and timeout mechanisms in the Mooncake connector. Add detection for failed prefill nodes and automatically trigger decode node state reset or failover to alternative prefill nodes when remote KV cache becomes unavailable.
Created: 2026-03-21 · View on GitHub →
#35943 [MooncakeConnector] Decode side requests stuck in WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS indefinitely on transfer failure, causing HBM leak
[Bug]: [MooncakeConnector] Decode side requests stuck in WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS indefinitely on transfer failure, causing HBM leak
Problem: In MooncakeConnector P/D disaggregation, when KV transfer from P to D fails, D-side requests remain stuck in WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS state permanently, causing progressive HBM leak as KV cache blocks are never freed.
Suggested Fix: Implement timeout mechanism for D-side WAITING_FOR_REMOTE_KVS state similar to P-side timeout handling. Add proper cleanup logic to release KV cache blocks when transfers fail or timeout.
Created: 2026-03-04 · View on GitHub →

6 NIXL handshake race condition when same-node workers re-register with new engine IDs #38840 NIXL handshake race when same-node workers re-register with new engine IDs

16 Add Mooncake Store Connector for shared KV cache reuse in production serving #38474 Add Mooncake Store Connector for Shared KV Cache Reuse
[RFC]: Add Mooncake Store Connector for Shared KV Cache Reuse
Problem: vLLM lacks shared KV cache reuse across instances. Multiple vLLM instances recompute identical KV blocks independently, and evicted cache blocks must be recomputed from scratch, wasting computation especially for common prompt prefixes.
Suggested Fix: Implement Mooncake Store Connector to enable distributed KV cache sharing. Design content-addressable storage using block hashes, add cache lookup before prefill computation, and implement cache population after successful prefill to enable cross-instance reuse.
Created: 2026-03-29 · View on GitHub →

18 Support routed_experts export in disaggregated Prefill/Decode serving #38523 Support `routed_experts` export in disaggregated Prefill/Decode serving
[Feature]: Support `routed_experts` export in disaggregated Prefill/Decode serving
Problem: `enable_return_routed_experts` works for single-instance deployments but fails in disaggregated Prefill/Decode serving because routed expert data is based on local memory and KV slot mapping, while PD transfer only carries `kv_transfer_params`.
Suggested Fix: Extend the PD protocol to transfer routed expert data across the prefill/decode boundary. Add routed expert information to the transfer parameters and implement logic to merge prefill and decode routed experts in the final response output.

#35848 Revamp Ray Distributed Executor Backend (from Ray team)

Problem: Current Ray distributed executor backend using Compiled Graph has performance issues and complexity. Ray team proposes replacing with simpler actor-based approach using ZMQ/SHM for control plane and NCCL for data plane.
Suggested Fix: Implement RayExecutorV2 following MultiprocExecutor communication model. Replace Compiled Graph with plain Ray actors and use established communication patterns for better maintainability.

#39069 Gemma4 tool parser truncates string values containing internal quotes

Suggested Fix: Modify the parsing logic in vllm/tool_parsers/gemma4_utils.py to handle quoted strings properly. Either keep the <|"> delimiters and parse them natively, or implement proper string literal parsing that handles escaped quotes and nested quotes correctly.

Problem: prompt_logprobs ignores the logprobs_mode configuration setting, always returning the same format regardless of whether logprobs_mode is set to 'raw_logits', 'raw_logprobs', or 'processed_logits'.
Suggested Fix: Update the prompt_logprobs implementation to respect the logprobs_mode setting. Ensure prompt logprobs processing follows the same mode-specific formatting as completion logprobs.
Created: 2026-03-03 · View on GitHub →
#36660 Duplicate token with different logprob when requesting top logprobs
[Bug]: Duplicate token with different logprob when requesting top
Problem: OpenAI chat completion API returns duplicate tokens with different logprobs in top_logprobs when requesting top_logprobs=5, showing the same token 'none' twice with different probabilities.
Suggested Fix: Fix the logprobs deduplication logic to prevent the same token from appearing multiple times with different probabilities. Ensure top-k logprobs selection properly handles token uniqueness.
Created: 2026-03-10 · View on GitHub →
#27300 vLLM produces invalid UTF-8 tokens and replacement characters when returning logprobs
[Bug]: vLLM produces invalid UTF-8 tokens and “�” replacement characters when returning logprobs
Problem: vLLM returns invalid UTF-8 tokens and replacement characters (�) in logprobs output, causing encoding issues when processing token probabilities.
Suggested Fix: Investigate the token decoding logic in the logprobs generation pipeline. Ensure proper UTF-8 validation and handle partial or invalid byte sequences correctly when converting token IDs to strings.
Created: 2025-10-21 · View on GitHub →
#34417 Need logprobs support for gpt-oss harmony models
[Feature]: logprobs for gpt-oss harmony
Problem: GPT-OSS Harmony models don't support logprobs output in the response serving implementation.
Suggested Fix: Extend the Harmony response serving logic to include logprobs calculation and formatting. Modify the response generation pipeline to capture and return token probabilities in the expected format for GPT-OSS models.
Created: 2026-02-12 · View on GitHub →
Bug Qwen GPT
11 Missing API endpoint features — /health/ready for GPU health, configuration API, request completion hooks #36960 Add /health/ready endpoint for GPU health verification beyond process alive check
[Feature]: Add /health/ready endpoint for GPU health verification
Problem: vLLM's /health endpoint only checks process liveness but doesn't verify GPU functionality. When GPU encounters page faults or illegal memory access, the process remains alive but inference fails, causing Kubernetes to route traffic to broken pods.
Suggested Fix: Implement /health/ready endpoint that performs actual GPU forward pass (1-token dummy batch) to verify end-to-end inference capability. This provides proper Kubernetes readiness probe functionality separate from liveness checks.
Created: 2026-03-13 · View on GitHub →
#38147 Add Configuration API to expose structured inference configuration
[RFC]: Add Configuration API
Problem: Feature request to add a Configuration API endpoint (GET /v1/config) that exposes structured inference configuration for distributed serving platforms like llm-d for prefix-cache aware routing.
Suggested Fix: Implement a new REST endpoint that returns static server configuration including model info (served names, dtype, max_model_len), KV cache settings (num_blocks, cache_dtype, prefix_caching), and parallelism configuration. Design the response schema to be easily consumable by routing systems.
Created: 2026-03-25 · View on GitHub →
#37086 Expose stable request completion hook in streaming serving paths
[Feature]: Expose stable request completion hook in streaming serving paths
Problem: No stable request completion hook exposed for streaming requests across multiple entrypoints. Completion detection occurs inside Python generator control flow when checking for [DONE] sentinel, making it difficult to add lifecycle hooks.
Suggested Fix: Add standardized completion hooks in the streaming serving layer before the [DONE] sentinel check. Implement hooks in base streaming classes that can be inherited by Anthropic, OpenAI, and other serving entrypoints.
Created: 2026-03-15 · View on GitHub →
#36189 Per-request timing headers via --enable-request-stats-headers flag
[Feature]: Per-Request Timing Headers (`--enable-request-stats-headers`)
Problem: Feature request for per-request timing headers via --enable-request-stats-headers flag. Users want to see timing breakdown (queue time, prefill time, decode time) for individual requests via HTTP response headers.
Suggested Fix: This is a feature request. Implement optional response headers that expose per-request timing metrics already tracked internally by vLLM. Add server flag to enable this feature and ensure headers are only added to non-streaming completion responses.
Created: 2026-03-06 · View on GitHub →

12 Model-specific deployment issues — chat template problems, embedding endpoint 404s, task parameter errors #38885 Potential misalignment between qwen3.5 chat template and recommended tool parser

Suggested Fix: Update qwen3coder_tool_parser to handle both JSON and Python string representations of None, or modify the chat template to consistently use tojson filter for all argument types.

#38531 Support direct binary/multipart file upload for video and image in OpenAI-compatible API
[Feature]: Support direct binary/multipart file upload for video and image in OpenAI-compatible API
Problem: The OpenAI-compatible API only accepts media via URLs or base64 encoding, but lacks support for direct binary/multipart file upload. Base64 encoding large videos (18MB → 24MB) exceeds shell ARG_MAX limits.
Suggested Fix: Implement a /v1/files upload endpoint or add multipart form support to /v1/chat/completions. This requires extending the FastAPI request handlers to accept multipart/form-data, storing uploaded files temporarily, and modifying the media processing pipeline to handle local file references.

#37344 Pooling API: expose extra_kwargs and allow nested response data for custom poolers
Pooling API: expose extra_kwargs and allow nested response data for custom poolers
Problem: Custom pooling workloads (like GLiNER-style span outputs) require structured metadata and nested tensor outputs that aren't supported by current PoolingRequest and PoolingResponseData typing constraints.
Suggested Fix: Add extra_kwargs parameter to PoolingCompletionRequest and PoolingChatRequest that passes through to PoolingParams. Relax PoolingResponseData.data typing to allow nested 3D tensor serialization for structured prediction head outputs like [L, K, C] logits.

#38651 Add max_tokens_per_doc support for rerank and scoring endpoints

Problem: vLLM's reranking and scoring endpoints lack `max_tokens_per_doc` parameter to truncate long documents before processing. When documents exceed context window, requests fail or produce degraded results, unlike Cohere and Jina APIs that support this feature.
Suggested Fix: Implement `max_tokens_per_doc` parameter in rerank/score request schema that truncates document tokens before model processing. Integrate with PoolingParams and ensure compatibility with offline scoring and score templates.

Feature Request
16 Monitoring and logging improvements — ANSI color disable, Prometheus buckets, error logging redesign #25943 Disable ANSI colors in logs of OpenAI compatible server
[Feature]: Disable ANSII colors in logs of OpenAI compatible server
Problem: No option to disable ANSI color escape sequences in OpenAI compatible server logs, causing log pollution in deployment environments like runpod that don't properly render colors.
Suggested Fix: Add command line flag (e.g., --no-color) to disable ANSI escape sequences in logging output. Modify the logging configuration to conditionally include color formatting based on this flag or detect TTY environments.
Created: 2025-09-30 · View on GitHub →
#34370 Make Prometheus histogram buckets configurable
[Feature]: Make Prometheus histogram buckets configurable
Problem: Prometheus histogram buckets for vLLM metrics (e.g., vllm:e2e_request_latency_seconds) are statically defined in source code, requiring custom patches for production deployments with different SLO requirements.
Suggested Fix: Add CLI flag, environment variable, or configuration file option to specify custom histogram bucket distributions for Prometheus metrics, allowing runtime configuration without code changes.
Created: 2026-02-11 · View on GitHub →
#31683 Error logging redesign for multiprocess architecture
[Feature]: Error Logging Redesign
Problem: Error logging in vLLM's multiprocess architecture (API Server -> EngineCore -> Workers) makes debugging difficult as the API server error is often not the root cause, which occurs in the worker processes.
Suggested Fix: Implement a centralized error logging system that propagates detailed error information from workers back to the API server. Add structured error types with root cause attribution and worker process identification to help users identify the actual source of failures.
Created: 2026-01-04 · View on GitHub →
#30956 Output given format logger support
[Feature]: could output the given format logger ?
Problem: User wants to integrate custom logger configuration file with vLLM CLI commands to use custom logging formats instead of default logging.
Suggested Fix: vLLM doesn't currently support custom logger file injection via CLI. Consider using environment variables to configure logging (VLLM_LOGGING_LEVEL) or modify the logging configuration in the vLLM source code. Alternatively, capture and redirect vLLM output through an external logging wrapper.
Created: 2025-12-18 · View on GitHub →
#31809 Add default logger to write to file option
[Feature]: Add a default logger select write to file
Problem: vLLM serve only writes logs to stdout and lacks built-in file logging option, requiring custom VLLM_LOGGING_CONFIG_PATH configuration for file output.
Suggested Fix: Add command-line options for log file output, including --log-file parameter and optional log rotation based on size/date. Implement default logging configuration that supports both stdout and file output.

#5 Installation / Build / CI

112 issues 17 clusters

Missing Blackwell RTX 50-series support in prebuilt wheels blocks user access to new hardware, while CI automation roadmap goals remain on track.

# Problem Related Issues Tags
1 Blackwell RTX 50-series (SM120/SM121) support missing in prebuilt wheels and Docker images #35432 Prebuilt vLLM wheels / official images fail on RTX 50-series (Blackwell, SM120/SM121) — "no kernel image" / "sm_120 not compatible"
Prebuilt vLLM wheels / official images fail on RTX 50-series (Blackwell, SM120/SM121) — "no kernel image" / "sm_120 not compatible"
Problem: vLLM prebuilt PyPI wheels and official Docker images fail on RTX 50-series GPUs (Blackwell, SM120/SM121) with 'no kernel image' or 'sm_120 not compatible' errors because they were built without Blackwell architecture flags.
Suggested Fix: Rebuild prebuilt wheels and Docker images with SM120/SM121 CUDA compute capability flags to support Blackwell architecture. Update the build pipeline to include these newer GPU architectures in the compilation targets.
Created: 2026-02-26 · View on GitHub →
#36865 SM120 / RTX 5090 source build still registers unsupported FlashMLA / FA targets and uses non-SM120 Marlin defaults
[Bug]: SM120 / RTX 5090 source build still registers unsupported FlashMLA / FA targets and uses non-SM120 Marlin defaults.
Problem: RTX 5090 (SM120/compute capability 12.0) source build incorrectly registers unsupported FlashMLA/FA targets and uses non-SM120 Marlin defaults, despite having working SM120 kernels for CUTLASS, NVFP4, and MLA.
Suggested Fix: Update build configuration to properly detect SM120 and disable FlashMLA targets (which only work on Hopper). Force SM120-specific Marlin kernel compilation and ensure FA2_ARCHS filtering excludes unsupported attention backends for Blackwell architecture.
Created: 2026-03-12 · View on GitHub →
#36821 No sm_121 (Blackwell) support on aarch64 — NVIDIA DGX Spark / Acer GN100
[Bug]: No sm_121 (Blackwell) support on aarch64 — NVIDIA DGX Spark / Acer GN100
Problem: vLLM fails to start on NVIDIA DGX Spark with GB10 Blackwell (sm_121) on aarch64 because bundled PyTorch only includes CUDA kernels up to sm_120. The GPU is detected but no compatible kernels exist for sm_121 compute capability.
Suggested Fix: Update build configuration to include sm_121 targets for Blackwell architecture. Ensure PyTorch is built with TORCH_CUDA_ARCH_LIST including sm_121 for aarch64 builds, and update CI to test Blackwell compatibility.

[Bug] V1 engine hangs on encoder cache profiling on AMD gfx1151 (MIOpen missing solver DB)
Problem: vLLM V1 engine hangs indefinitely during encoder cache profiling on AMD gfx1151 (Radeon 8060S) with vision models. MIOpen convolution operations never complete due to missing solver database metadata for gfx1151 architecture.
Suggested Fix: Check if gfx1151 is properly supported in the ROCm/MIOpen installation. Either update ROCm to a version with gfx1151 solver databases, use --enforce-eager to bypass problematic profiling, or implement fallback logic when MIOpen metadata is missing for new GPU architectures.

Problem: vLLM startup hangs at 'Waiting for 1 local, 0 remote core engine proc(s) to start' on AMD AI MAX+ 395 GPU with ROCm 7.2 on Ubuntu 24.04, with one CPU thread at 100% usage indefinitely.

Consider testing with simpler models or enabling more debug logging to isolate the hang point.

#35642 HIP build in Docker: offload-arch stderr contaminates compiler flags via cmake/utils.cmake and CMAKE_HIP_FLAGS
[Bug]: HIP build in Docker: offload-arch stderr contaminates compiler flags via cmake/utils.cmake and CMAKE_HIP_FLAGS
Problem: HIP build in Docker fails because offload-arch stderr output contaminates compiler flags via cmake/utils.cmake, causing clang++ to interpret warning strings as filenames when building without GPU passthrough.
Suggested Fix: Separate stderr from stdout in offload-arch execution and sanitize compiler flag extraction in cmake/utils.cmake to prevent warning messages from being interpreted as compiler arguments.

8 WSL2 / Windows installation and process management issues #39093 WSL2: Ctrl+C shows shutdown complete but requires pkill -9 (lingering processes)
[Bug] WSL2: Ctrl+C shows shutdown complete but requires pkill -9 (lingering processes)
Problem: On WSL2, Ctrl+C shows shutdown complete messages but vLLM processes remain alive, requiring manual pkill -9. The high-level shutdown appears successful but child/worker processes aren't properly terminated.
Suggested Fix: Improve signal handling on WSL2 by ensuring SIGINT propagates to all child processes. Add explicit process group termination and verify that distributed processes, NCCL workers, and CUDA context cleanup complete properly. Consider WSL2-specific process management differences.

Bug
13 Dependency version conflicts and package compatibility issues #36676 dependency `nixl<0.10.0` installs `nixl-cu12==0.10.1`
[Bug]: dependency `nixl<0.10.0` installs `nixl-cu12==0.10.1`
Problem: vLLM's dependency pinning `nixl>=0.7.1,<0.10.0` fails because nixl is a meta-package that directs to `nixl-cu12>=@VERSION@` instead of `nixl-cu12==@VERSION@`, causing nixl-cu12==0.10.1 to be installed despite the version constraint.
Suggested Fix: Update the dependency specification to pin the actual backend packages (nixl-cu12, nixl-cu13) directly instead of relying on the meta-package, or modify the version constraint logic to handle the meta-package's dependency resolution behavior.

Feature Request ROCm NVIDIA
16 Build source compilation errors and ABI compatibility issues #38420 _C_stable_libtorch fails to build: const& references violate stable ABI trivially_copyable requirement
[Bug]: _C_stable_libtorch fails to build: const& references violate stable ABI trivially_copyable requirement
Problem: _C_stable_libtorch fails to build with const reference violations in stable ABI. Static assertion failures occur because const torch::stable::Tensor& references are not trivially copyable, violating stable ABI requirements that need memcpy-safe types.
Suggested Fix: Change function signatures in csrc/libtorch_stable/ops.h from const torch::stable::Tensor& to torch::stable::Tensor (pass-by-value) to ensure trivially copyable types across the stable ABI boundary. Update all affected function signatures.

#6 Performance

51 issues 19 clusters

Significant throughput regressions on flagship models like Qwen3.5-397B and Gemma 4 undermine vLLM's performance leadership positioning.

# Problem Related Issues Tags
1 Qwen3.5-397B NVFP4 decode throughput regression (-19%) on 8×B200 over 10 days — likely v0.19.0 regression #39004 ~23% output throughput regression on Qwen3.5-397B NVFP4 decode (8×B200) over the last 10 days
[Perf]: ~23% output throughput regression on Qwen3.5-397B NVFP4 decode (8×B200) over the last 10 days
Problem: ~23% output throughput regression for nvidia/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-NVFP4 on 8×B200 GPUs, dropping from ~47,200 to ~38,300 tok/s between March 28-April 4. The flashinfer upgrade from 0.6.6 to 0.6.7 coincides with the largest drop.
Suggested Fix: Revert to flashinfer 0.6.6 to confirm if the upgrade caused the regression. Profile the attention kernel performance differences between versions. Check for changes in memory layout, kernel fusion, or batch processing that might impact MoE throughput on B200.

2 Gemma 4 E4B extremely slow on v0.19.0 forced TRITON_ATTN fallback yields ~9 tok/s on RTX 4090 (vs ~100+ tok/s for comparable Llama 3B) #38887 Gemma 4 E4B extremely slow on v0.19.0 forced TRITON_ATTN fallback yields ~9 tok/s on RTX 4090 (vs ~100+ tok/s for comparable Llama 3B)

17 Speculative decoding and CUDA graph optimization opportunities #36657 Dynamic Speculation Length (DSL) with Confidence-Threshold Early Exit for vLLM Speculative Decoding
[RFC]: Dynamic Speculation Length (DSL) with Confidence-Threshold Early Exit for vLLM Speculative Decoding
Problem: Standard speculative decoding runs draft model for fixed num_speculative_tokens steps regardless of confidence, wasting compute on uncertain tokens. Dynamic Speculation Length (DSL) proposal aims to adapt draft length based on confidence thresholds.
Suggested Fix: Implement the proposed draft_confidence_threshold field in SpeculativeConfig and modify DraftModelProposer to evaluate early exit conditions based on batch-level mean confidence scores.
Created: 2026-03-10 · View on GitHub →
#33341 [Feature][Performance][Speculative Decoding]: Support Full CUDA Graph for the drafter
[Feature][Performance][Speculative Decoding]: Support Full CUDA Graph for the drafter
Problem: Drafter in speculative decoding (eagle.py) only supports piecewise CUDA graphs, lacking full CUDA graph support which would improve performance by ~5% TPOT.
Suggested Fix: Implement full CUDA graph support for drafter by making buffers static (like input_ids, positions). Add proper KV-cache buffer management and slot_mapping handling for graph capture.
Created: 2026-01-29 · View on GitHub →
#33456 [Feature][Speculative Decoding]: Consolidate EAGLE Input Preparation
[Feature][Speculative Decoding]: Consolidate EAGLE Input Preparation
Problem: EAGLE's input preparation process is scattered across multiple methods (prepare_next_token_ids, prepare_input_ids, set_inputs_first_pass) owned by different callers, creating complexity and redundant work.
Suggested Fix: Refactor EAGLE's input preparation into a single prepare_inputs() method called from propose(). This requires redesigning the proposer class interface to reduce dependency on model runner state and enable direct buffer writing.
Created: 2026-01-31 · View on GitHub →
#33455 [Feature][Performance][Speculative Decoding]: Kernel Fusion for EAGLE Steps
[Feature][Performance][Speculative Decoding]: Kernel Fusion for EAGLE Steps
Problem: EAGLE speculative decoding issues many small kernels between steps for updating slot mapping and metadata, creating overhead in the drafting process.
Suggested Fix: Implement kernel fusion to combine multiple small update operations into a single Triton kernel. Alternatively, apply torch.compile on the entire autoregressive drafting portion to enable broader optimizations and potential CUDA graph fusion.

11 Add support for advanced quantization methods: TurboQuant, RotorQuant, ParoQuant for KV cache compression #38291 Add Rotorquant support

Problem: Request to add RotorQuant support for KV cache compression. RotorQuant uses Clifford algebra-based rotors instead of dense rotation matrices, providing 10-19x faster quantization with 44x fewer parameters while maintaining similar accuracy to TurboQuant.
Suggested Fix: Implement RotorQuant integration following existing KV cache quantization patterns. Study the Clifford algebra implementation and integration points for KV cache compression in vLLM.

#35792 Support MLA attention + quant fusions

Problem: MLA attention lacks fusion support with quantization methods. Phase 1 has static FP8/NVFP4 support, but group FP8 fusion and output quantization for various kernel paths (FlashInfer, FlashAttn, TRT-LLM, CuDNN) remain unimplemented.
Suggested Fix: Complete Phase 2 implementation by adding output quantization support for all MLA attention kernel paths. Work with upstream kernel maintainers to add quantized output support in FlashInfer, FlashAttn, and other attention backends.

16 KV cache quantization enhancements: INT8 support, per-channel scaling, and NVFP4 compatibility #33480 Add INT8 Support for KV Cache Quantization (Currently FP8-Only)
[Feature]: Add INT8 Support for KV Cache Quantization (Currently FP8-Only)
Problem: vLLM only supports FP8 quantization for KV Cache but lacks INT8 support. FP8 requires SM9.0+ GPUs (H100+), making it incompatible with older hardware like A100 (SM8.0) or consumer GPUs.
Suggested Fix: Implement INT8 KV cache quantization by adding an INT8 backend to the existing quantization infrastructure. Create INT8-specific kernels for older GPU architectures and add configuration options to select between FP8 and INT8 quantization based on hardware capabilities.

Bug
▾

#8 Multimodality

38 issues 24 clusters

MTP concurrency crashes and encoder issues affect the streaming inputs roadmap milestone but have more limited user base than core engine issues.

# Problem Related Issues Tags
1 MTP + multimodal crashes with encoder cache misses under high concurrency — blocks streaming inputs roadmap milestone #38551 AssertionError: Encoder cache miss crashes engine with MTP + multimodal under high concurrency
[Bug]: AssertionError: Encoder cache miss crashes engine with MTP + multimodal under high concurrency
Problem: MTP speculative decoding with multimodal inputs crashes under high concurrency when encoder cache entries are evicted before MTP draft proposer reads them. AssertionError 'Encoder cache miss' crashes entire engine, distinct from previous EAGLE3 cache issues.
Suggested Fix: Implement graceful handling for encoder cache misses in MTP draft proposal. Either increase encoder cache size for high concurrency workloads, implement cache miss fallback logic, or coordinate cache eviction with draft proposer lifecycle to prevent mid-flight evictions.

6 Batch invariant aten overrides bypassed under torch.compile causing RMSNorm failures #39096 Batch invariant aten::IMPL overrides bypassed under torch.compile for RMSNorm

RFC/Discussion Qwen GLM CUDA
12 Implement vLLM IR functional representation for custom operations compilation #38744 Automatically compile native impl for IR ops
[RFC][vLLM IR]: Automatically compile native impl for IR ops
### Motivation. Sometimes we need to call an IR op inside another opaque torch custom op. That means the IR op will be invisible to model-level compilation, and dispatching to the raw native implementation will hurt performance. This problem is not unique to vLLM IR; it happens for `CustomOp` instances as well, and we currently circumvent it by wrapping `forward_native` with `torch.compile`. Prime examples of this are `SiluAndMul` and `QuantFP8` inside `fused_moe`. The same mechanism is utiliz...
Created: 2026-04-01 · View on GitHub →
#32358 vLLM IR: A Functional Intermediate Representation for vLLM
[RFC]: vLLM IR: A Functional Intermediate Representation for vLLM
Problem: vLLM's CustomOp-based kernel dispatching has compilation issues and clunky dispatching logic. torch.compile passes struggle with custom operators like RMSNorm and Quant that decompose to fragile torch ops sequences.
Suggested Fix: Implement the proposed vLLM IR functional intermediate representation to replace CustomOp dispatching. This would provide better torch.compile integration and more maintainable kernel selection logic.
Created: 2026-01-14 · View on GitHub →
RFC/Discussion
13 Unwrap FusedMoE custom ops to enable better torch.compile optimization #31985 Unwrap FusedMoE custom op to prevent torch.compile barriers from chunking
[Feature]: Unwrap FusedMoE custom op
Problem: FusedMoE layer is wrapped in custom ops preventing torch.compile optimizations, increasing CPU overhead and causing inefficiencies. The SiluMul custom op within FusedMoE uses uncompiled forward_native, degrading performance, and the layer name interferes with compile caching.
Suggested Fix: Unwrap the FusedMoE custom op to expose individual operations to torch.compile. Refactor DP chunking logic and replace custom ops like SiluMul with native torch operations that can be automatically compiled to efficient kernels.
Created: 2026-01-08 · View on GitHub →

RFC/Discussion
17 Optimize compilation performance with better Dynamo capture and partitioning #33645 Improve Dynamo compile times 5-10x via unsafe assumptions
[RFC]: Improve Dynamo compile times 5-10x via unsafe assumptions
Problem: Dynamo compilation times could be improved 5-10x by avoiding repeated tracing of identical Transformer blocks in decoder models through unsafe assumptions.
Suggested Fix: Implement an 'unsafe_dynamo_once' decorator that reuses the first traced block for subsequent identical layers. Audit models to identify safe candidates for this optimization while ensuring correctness.
Created: 2026-02-03 · View on GitHub →
#27080 To Inductor partition or to not Inductor partition by default
[RFC]: To Inductor partition or to not Inductor partition (by default in v0.11.1)
Problem: Decision needed on enabling Inductor partitioning by default in v0.11.1 - it improves performance but increases compile time and has compatibility risks with torch 2.9.
Suggested Fix: Evaluate trade-offs between performance gains and compile time increases. Consider making Inductor partitioning optional with clear documentation, or enable by default with fallback mechanisms for compatibility issues.
Created: 2025-10-17 · View on GitHub →
#27828 Inductor partition performance issues
[Performance][torch.compile]: Inductor partition performance issues
Problem: Inductor partition performance is inconsistent - sometimes slower than Dynamo partition or no partition. On Blackwell, no partition can be faster than Inductor partition, and on Hopper, Inductor partition appears slower than Dynamo partition.
Suggested Fix: Profile and compare kernel fusion effectiveness between FULL_AND_PIECEWISE vs FULL_DECODE_ONLY cudagraph modes. Benchmark Inductor vs Dynamo partition on single-TP setups to isolate the performance regression and analyze attention+quantization fusion patterns on different architectures.
Created: 2025-10-30 · View on GitHub →

# Problem Related Issues Tags
1 Modular weight sync API implementation - BLOCKS core roadmap objective for RL rollout infrastructure #31848 Native Weight Syncing APIs RFC with NCCL and IPC implementation progress

Problem: RFC for implementing native weight syncing APIs in vLLM with NCCL and IPC support for dynamic model weight updates during runtime.

2 Batch invariance support for A100 hardware and broader model compatibility - extends current H100+ limitation #32658 Batch invariance feature request for A100 GPUs
[Feature]: batch invariance for A100
Problem: Batch invariance feature is limited to compute capability 9.0+ GPUs (H100, H200, B100, B200) and doesn't support A100 GPUs which have compute capability 8.0.
Suggested Fix: Investigate architectural requirements for batch invariance on compute capability 8.0. May require different implementation approach or kernel optimizations specific to A100 architecture.

Problem: Batch Invariant feature tracking issue for various components including framework, model support, and optimizations across different GPU architectures.

#30321 Batch invariant support needed for DP+EP configurations with Qwen models
[Feature]: Batch Invariant Feature in DP+EP
Problem: Batch invariant feature works with tensor parallelism but shows inconsistent results when using data parallel + expert parallel (DP+EP), except when DP=2.
Suggested Fix: Investigate expert routing and load balancing algorithms in DP+EP setup. Check if expert assignment or gradient synchronization differs between DP sizes, causing batch-dependent behavior.

3 Enhanced pause/resume API for in-flight requests and DPEP scenarios - supports RL weight update workflows #32103 Extending vLLM Pause/Resume API to support in-flight requests and DPEP scenarios
[RFC]: Extending vLLM Pause/Resume API to support in-flight requests and DPEP scenarios
Problem: RFC for extending vLLM pause/resume API to support online RL weight updates with in-flight requests and DPEP scenarios. Needs three pause modes: abort, finish, keep for zero-downtime updates.
Suggested Fix: Implement the proposed pause/resume API design with keep mode for preserving request state during weight updates. Focus on scheduler loop control and cache management decoupling for DPEP compatibility.

5 ZMQ socket port conflicts in distributed RL configurations causing DeepSeek-V3 deployment failures #28498 Port conflict bug in 2P 4D configuration for DeepSeek-V3 with ZMQ sockets
[Bug][RL]: Port Conflict
Problem: ZMQ socket port conflict bug occurs in 2P 4D configuration for DeepSeek-V3 using vLLM v0.11.0, causing crashes in distributed setups even on clean nodes.
Suggested Fix: Implement proper port allocation and cleanup for ZMQ sockets in distributed configurations. Add port conflict detection and automatic port assignment to prevent collisions in multi-process setups.

6 LM head FP32 casting support for stable logprobs in RLHF training workflows #19925 Support casting lm_head to FP32 to get old logprobs in RLHF
[Feature]: Support casting lm_head to FP32 to get old logprobs in RLHF
Problem: Request for feature to cast lm_head to FP32 during RLHF training to maintain numerical precision for logprobs computation, as suggested in research papers.
Suggested Fix: Implement a configuration option to selectively cast the lm_head layer to FP32 while keeping other model components in lower precision. Add this as a model loading parameter.
